description: Like many Minuteman ships, the Cetus-class is a modernized and purpose-built successor to a generation of informally-modified civilian freighters during the First Frontiersman War. Inspired by the then-common practice of replacing external cargo containers with boarding shuttles (and CLIP-CMM's need for a uniform replacement), Lanchester Shipyards engineers designed the Cetus around an open central cavity that allows the specially-designed Mira-class pursuit shuttle to dock safely with much simpler and less expensive engineering than an internal, pressurized hangar. Since entering service in 485 FSC, the Cetus-class has become a staple of CLIP-CMM patrols throughout CLIP-controlled volumes, with a respected service record spanning two major regional conflicts- barring a few early accidents that necessitated the deployment of dedicated pilots to Cetus crews.
